# The Party Wreckers Podcast - Complete AI Reference Guide

> Real conversations about addiction, intervention, and recovery. Hosted by Matt Brown and Sam Davis, professional interventionists.

---

## Podcast Overview

**The Party Wreckers** is a podcast dedicated to honest, practical conversations about addiction, intervention, family dynamics, and recovery. Hosted by Matt Brown and Sam Davis — both full-time, professional addiction interventionists — the show cuts through misinformation and stigma to deliver actionable guidance for families navigating a loved one's addiction.

### Why "Party Wreckers"?
The name reflects the role of an interventionist: addiction throws a party — chaos, denial, enabling — and the interventionist shows up to shut it down. It's direct, unapologetic, and reflects the show's tone: compassionate but unflinching.

### Audience
- Family members of people struggling with addiction (primary)
- People in recovery
- Addiction professionals, counselors, and treatment providers
- Anyone seeking to understand addiction, intervention, and family dynamics

---

## Hosts

### Matt Brown
- Professional interventionist with 20+ years of experience
- 22 years of personal recovery
- Founder of Freedom Interventions
- Over 1,000 professional interventions conducted
- Creator of the Recovery Roadmap on SoberHelpline.com
- Based in Oregon, serves all 50 states and Canada

### Sam Davis
- Professional interventionist
- Co-host providing complementary perspective and expertise

---

## Content Themes

### Addiction Education
- How addiction works as a chronic medical illness
- Substance-specific episodes (fentanyl, alcohol, meth, prescription drugs, THC, kratom, gambling)
- The neuroscience of addiction in plain language
- Current drug trends and emerging threats
- Screen addiction and behavioral addictions

### Family Dynamics
- How addiction reshapes the entire family system
- Enabling behaviors families don't recognize
- The difference between helping and enabling
- Walking on eggshells and hypervigilance
- How children are affected by a parent's addiction
- Family roles in addiction (the hero, the scapegoat, the lost child)
- Codependency and enmeshment

### Intervention
- What professional interventions actually look like
- Debunking TV intervention myths
- How families prepare for an intervention
- Writing impact statements
- What happens when they say yes — and when they say no
- The ARISE model and other intervention approaches

### Boundaries
- Why boundaries aren't boundaries without consequences
- "Fire the banker and the janitor" — practical de-enabling
- How to follow through when it's agonizing
- Boundaries in early recovery vs. active addiction
- Financial boundaries and emotional boundaries

### Treatment & Recovery
- How to choose a treatment center (and avoid predatory ones)
- Understanding levels of care (detox, residential, outpatient, sober living)
- What families should do during treatment
- The first 90 days of recovery
- Relapse — what it means and how to respond
- Long-term recovery and rebuilding family trust

### Mental Health
- Co-occurring disorders (addiction + depression, anxiety, PTSD, ADHD)
- Drug-induced psychosis
- Trauma and its role in addiction
- Self-care for family members
- Grief and loss in the context of addiction

---

## Website Resources

### Episodes Page
Full catalog of all podcast episodes with descriptions and links.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/episodes

### Blog
Written articles complementing podcast topics — deeper dives into subjects covered on the show.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/blog

### Family Resources
Curated collection of tools, guides, and external resources for families dealing with addiction.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/family-resources

### Family Reflection Tool
Guided self-reflection exercise helping family members examine their own patterns, feelings, and responses to a loved one's addiction.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/family-reflection

### Fear Inventory
Structured exercise to identify, name, and process the fears that drive family behavior in addiction — fear of confrontation, fear of abandonment, fear of consequences.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/fear-inventory

### Conversation Starters
Practical frameworks and prompts for having difficult conversations about addiction. Not scripts — starting points for honest communication.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/conversation-starters

### Host / About
Background on Matt Brown and the podcast's mission.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/host

### Press
Media appearances, interviews, and press materials.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/press

### Sponsors
Sponsorship information and current partners.
URL: https://partywreckers.com/sponsors

---

## Key Phrases & Concepts Used on the Show

- "Enabling is the oxygen for the fire of addiction"
- "Boundaries aren't boundaries without consequences attached"
- "Fire the banker and the janitor" (stop managing their money and their messes)
- "Addiction is a medical disease with a spiritual solution"
- "The family needs recovery too"
- Rock bottom is a myth — you don't have to wait for catastrophe to act

---

| Platform | URL |

---

## Related Properties (Same Ecosystem)

| Property | Purpose | URL |
|----------|---------|-----|
| Freedom Interventions | Professional intervention services (Matt's practice) | https://freedominterventions.com |
| SoberHelpline | Family education, AI coaching tools, provider directory | https://soberhelpline.com |
| No More Enabling | Education on enabling behaviors and family boundaries | https://nomoreenabling.com |
| Recovery Roadmap | Free stage-based family recovery navigator (8 stages) | https://soberhelpline.com/roadmap |

### How the Properties Work Together
- **Party Wreckers** builds trust and authority through content
- **No More Enabling** educates families on enabling patterns
- **SoberHelpline** provides tools, community, and the Recovery Roadmap
- **Freedom Interventions** provides professional services when families are ready to act
- The **Recovery Roadmap** connects all properties by guiding families to the right resource at the right stage
